title: Wire digikoma-recap into digikoma-winddown as the structured-output subroutine
description: digikoma-recap (just landed at digikoma-org/private/universal/domain/intelligence/digikoma-recap/) is the pure transcript-to-RecapModel summarizer — read-only, no persistence. digikoma-winddown's spec already exists at intelligence/digikoma-winddown/ but has no implementation. The clean implementation path: digikoma-winddown calls digikoma-recap.run(transcript) to produce a RecapModel, then persists each field to the active agent's surfaces — recap.title + arcs + wins -> chronicle entry contributions, recap.openFollowups[] -> bead writes (one .issue.json per followup), recap.caveats[] -> surface in the recap output and possibly mint repair-* beads automatically. recap.rulesEmerged[] could feed into proposed memory entries. Tests should cover: (a) winddown calls recap once, (b) every persistence target is written, (c) malformations in recap.caveats[] don't silently corrupt downstream writes, (d) re-running winddown is idempotent for the same recap object.
